title: ADR-012: PEV Agent System — Claude Code Native Implementation

sections.heading: Context
sections.content: The PEV (Planning → Execution → Validation) agent system was originally designed around LangGraph with Anthropic API and checkpoint DB dependencies. Claude Code now provides native primitives — agent definitions with `tools` + `hooks` frontmatter, skills, subagents, and worktree isolation — that make LangGraph unnecessary. Superpowers provides workflow discipline (brainstorming, TDD, code review) that PEV phases can reference as sub-skills.

sections.heading: Decision
sections.content:
Implement PEV as Claude Code agent definitions + skills + Superpowers integration. Three agents (`pev-architect`, `pev-builder`, `pev-auditor`) with two-layer deterministic tool enforcement (Layer 1: `tools:` allowlist strips unauthorized tools at runtime; Layer 2: per-agent `hooks:` PreToolUse exits 2 as defense in depth). One orchestrator skill (`/pev`) chains phases with human approval gates.

**Key decisions:**

1. **Two-layer deterministic enforcement** — No tool restriction relies on prompt instructions alone.
2. **Architect has doc-write tools** — Updates feature docs to target state + creates changelog entry. Cannot write code (Edit/Write/Bash blocked by hooks).
3. **Builder runs in isolated worktree** — Avoids collisions with other sessions. Does NOT commit. Orchestrator merges worktree after human approval.
4. **Auditor reviews all stale nodes** — Marks clean if agrees, sends back to Builder if not. Stale ≠ broken. Has `cortex_source` to read code for judgment, but cannot modify code.
5. **Commit at the end** — One commit after all phases complete and human approves. Code + docs + changelog together.
6. **Feature docs are ground truth** — PRDs and design specs are living docs updated in place. Changelog entries are the plan for scoped changes. ADRs for cross-cutting changes.
7. **Merge conflicts surfaced, never auto-resolved** — If worktree merge conflicts, the user decides.

sections.heading: Architecture Summary
sections.content:
```
User request
  → Architect (doc-write, no code) → scope decision → update feature docs + changelog
    → Architect validation (lightweight reviewer, max 2 loops)
    → Human gate
  → Builder (code-write, doc-read, worktree) → implement → TDD → manifest
    → Worktree merge (surface conflicts, never auto-resolve)
    → cortex_build + graph dependency check
    → Human gate
  → Auditor (doc-write, code-read, mark_clean) → review stale → update docs → impact report
    → needs_fix? → Builder loopback (worktree, max 2x) → Auditor re-check
    → Human gate
  → Commit (one commit, everything together)
  → superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch
```

| Phase | Agent Def | Worktree | Can Write Code | Can Write Docs | Can mark_clean |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Architect | pev-architect | No | No | Yes | No |
| Builder | pev-builder | Yes | Yes | No | No |
| Auditor | pev-auditor | No | No | Yes | Yes |

sections.heading: Implementation Plan
sections.content:
The detailed implementation plan with step-by-step tasks lives in the plan document. Each task is linked below.

| Task | Description | Plan Section |
|---|---|---|
| Task 1 | Create pev-architect agent + skill | See link |
| Task 2 | Create pev-builder agent + skill (worktree) | See link |
| Task 3 | Create pev-auditor agent + skill | See link |
| Task 4 | Create pev orchestrator skill | See link |
| Task 5 | Hooks evaluation (V1: none needed) | See link |
| Task 6 | Skill testing (TDD pressure tests) | See link |
| Task 7 | Update PEV feature docs + changelog | See link |

sections.heading: Status
sections.content: **Proposed.** Plan drafted and reviewed. Not yet implemented. Blocked on ADR-010 (nested DocJSON sections) completing first — PEV plan doc itself would benefit from section nesting.

sections.heading: Consequences
sections.content:
**Gains:**
- Zero external dependencies (no LangGraph, no Anthropic API, no checkpoint DB)
- Deterministic tool enforcement via hooks (not prompt persuasion)
- Native integration with Superpowers workflow skills
- Worktree isolation prevents Builder collisions
- Single end-of-cycle commit ensures nothing is half-committed

**Loses:**
- No programmatic checkpoint/resume across process restarts
- No conditional routing outside of skill prompts (all orchestration is in SKILL.md)
- Changelog-driven planning requires discipline in entry structure

**Risks:**
- Agent `tools:` frontmatter enforcement needs verification (is it truly runtime-enforced or advisory?)
- Large PEV sessions may hit context limits across subagent dispatches
- Auditor's stale-node review could be slow on large changesets
